behavior_summary: Provides the gameplay behavior for a 'theurgist' NPC: housekeeping when leaving an area, short examine responses, a defensive/alert reaction when 'cast' at/attacked, and a multi-branch conversation when 'used' (talked to).
U8P_THURGIST::leaveFastArea clears several area/region references that tie the NPC to a trigger and marks the process excluded so the area no longer treats the NPC as present.
U8P_THURGIST::look makes the NPC emit a short bark depending on whether it is dead ('dead theurgist ' vs 'theurgist ').

U8P_THURGIST::cast polls for a valid combat target (up to a short retry count), and if a hostile/valid target is found it triggers alert barks, spawns visual/sound effects, changes animation/frame state, and may teleport the target by random offsets.
U8P_THURGIST::use runs a random greeting, then inspects the NPC's frame/state and branches into dialogue/animation/activity choices (busy/unavailable responses, spawn animations, set activity).
notable_control_flow: U8P_THURGIST::leaveFastArea: straight-line cleanup — set info / exclude process, then call resetRef for multiple reference ids (0x0205, 0x0204, 0x00F0, 0x0200) passing the NPC pointer.
U8P_THURGIST::look: query Npc::isDead() and select one of two static bark strings, then suspend.

U8P_THURGIST::cast: initialize a small counter (BP-02 = 5) and loop polling Npc::getTarget() with short suspends (FREE::1C19) until a valid target or timeout; on success check target != self, target not dead, and Item::getStatus(target) has bit 0x10 before performing the alert/effect sequence, changing frames, spawning multiple effect methods, and applying Npc::teleport with urandom offsets.

U8P_THURGIST::use: choose greeting by urandom, spawn interaction animation/effect (METHOD::0BCF, FREE::1C19), then obtain Item::getFrame() (via loopscr evaluation) into BP-08 and branch on its value to select dialogue, spawn METHOD::1090 in branches and call Npc::cSetActivity to record post-conversation activity.
open_questions: Exact semantic meaning of the various 'info' ids set at the start of handlers (0x021C, 0x0208, 0x021D, 0x0207) — likely engine metadata/state markers but precise semantics unknown.
Precise game-level meanings of the resetRef reference ids (0x0205, 0x0204, 0x00F0, 0x0200): which map/area lists they correspond to is inferred but not certain.

Exact effects and parameters of METHOD::15E7, METHOD::0113, METHOD::0BCF, METHOD::1090 and FREE::* spawn codes — they appear to be visual/animation/effect spawns but their concrete in-game appearance or timing is not documented here.
The encoding/values of Item::getFrame() used by use() to drive dialogue branches: only relative behavior is clear (0,1 produce specific responses) but full frame enumeration is unknown.

main_function.name: U8P_THURGIST::leaveFastArea
main_function.purpose: When the NPC leaves a fast-area trigger, remove area/region references for that NPC and mark the process excluded so the area no longer considers the NPC present.
entry_conditions: Triggered by U8P_THURGIST::leaveFastArea event — interpreted as the actor leaving the function's trigger region (area exit).

function_map.name: U8P_THURGIST::leaveFastArea
function_map.role: Cleanup/reset references when the actor leaves a fast-area trigger; clear multiple resetRef slots and mark the process excluded.

function_map.name: U8P_THURGIST::look
function_map.role: Respond to examine/look interactions; choose a short bark depending on Npc::isDead() and suspend.

function_map.name: U8P_THURGIST::cast
function_map.role: Defensive/alert behavior invoked on a cast/attack-like event: poll for target, spawn effects, change frame/state, possibly teleport target.

function_map.name: U8P_THURGIST::use
function_map.role: Handle 'use' (talk/interact) event: random greeting, read frame state, branch on frame to select dialogue/animations and set NPC activity.

function_map.name: U8P_THURGIST::resetRef
function_map.role: Engine utility to clear/reset a reference entry tying a process to an area/slot; called with a reference id and the actor/process id.

function_map.name: U8P_THURGIST::set info (opcode 77)
function_map.role: Engine opcode to set process/item info/metadata used before marking process excluded.

function_map.name: U8P_THURGIST::process exclude (opcode 78)
function_map.role: Engine opcode to mark process excluded from some processing lists.

external_calls.purpose: Mark the process with an info id (0x021C) before exclusion; likely records U8P_THURGIST::leaveFastArea/area-state change.
Exclude the process from further processing in the area after leaving.
Clear multiple area/region reference slots for the NPC so it is no longer associated with the fast-area trigger (ids: 0x0205, 0x0204, 0x00F0, 0x0200).
Set info id 0x0208, probably to mark that the look handler ran or to set temporary state before exclusion.
Exclude process briefly while look handling occurs.
Decide which bark string to emit based on whether the NPC is dead.
Emit the short description/bark string ('dead theurgist ' or 'theurgist ').
Set info id 0x021D before exclusion/behavior sequence.
Exclude process during cast handling.
Poll for a valid target to react to; looped with a small counter and short suspend waits.
Read status bits of the target (test bit 0x10) to decide whether to perform the alert reaction.
Emit an alarm/help bark when a valid hostile target is found.
Spawn various visual/sound/timed effects and a short wait while performing the reaction sequence.
Read and update the NPC frame/state during the reaction sequence.
Generate random offsets used in one call to Npc::teleport for variability in teleport behavior.
Apply a random teleport offset to the target as part of the reaction.
Set info id 0x0207 and exclude the process before handling conversation/interaction.
Exclude process during use handling.
Choose between two greeting phrases with a probabilistic split.
Speak the chosen greeting or subsequent dialogue lines to the player.
Spawn animation/effect helpers to accompany conversation branches.
Obtain the NPC's frame/state (stored to BP-08) to select dialogue branches.
Set the NPC's activity/state after a dialogue branch completes (observed setting to activity 0 in branches).
Cleanup/free certain ids/resources as part of preparing the interaction state.

quick_facts.behavior_summary_one_liner: Provides the gameplay behavior for a 'theurgist' NPC: housekeeping when leaving an area, short examine responses, a defensive/alert reaction when 'cast' at/attacked, and a multi-branch conversation when 'used' (talked to).
